decision.semantic_admission_delta: positive for focused JS/TS imported Promise producer fixtures; unchanged on the local crates runtime surface because crates has no JS/TS Promise continuation rows
decision.summary: Add a reusable PromiseSettledValue evidence capability so imported Promise producers can recover a settled payload only when imported call-target identity, PromiseLike receiver proof, continuation API evidence, and a settled channel/payload contract all compose.
policy: Imported CallTarget evidence remains target identity only; it does not imply Promise settlement.
PromiseSettledValue is anchored to the exact imported producer Call node and records Fulfilled or Rejected plus the exact payload span/kind.
The semantic resolver admits the contract only for builtin, dependency-closed, unambiguous evidence paired with admitted ImportedFunction or ImportedMember call-target identity.
Promise continuation recovery still requires Domain(PromiseLike) receiver proof and admitted Promise .then/.catch/.finally LibraryApi evidence.
Fulfilled imported payload contracts remain closed unless the value graph can prove the payload non-thenable-safe; rejected reasons do not assimilate thenables.
The recovered result remains behind a Promise boundary and does not converge with synchronous payload code.

kernel_capabilities: nose-il now has EvidenceKind::PromiseSettledValue with PromiseSettlementChannel and payload span/kind fields
nose-semantics exposes a fail-closed promise_settled_value_evidence_at_call resolver that requires imported call-target identity and a unique payload node
the Promise value graph consumes admitted imported settled-value contracts inside the existing PromiseLike receiver proof path
contractless imported producers and fulfilled contracts over possible thenable payloads remain opaque
newly_supported_equivalences: contract-backed imported load(1).then(x => x + 1) recovers to the same Promise payload as Promise.resolve(1).then(x => x + 1), while preserving the Promise boundary
contract-backed imported rejected load(1).catch(e => e + 1) recovers to a fulfilled Promise boundary over the catch payload
still_closed_equivalences: ordinary source-level imported Promise producers without PromiseSettledValue evidence
imported fulfilled contracts whose payload may be thenable
settled-value evidence without admitted imported call-target identity
direct, direct-method, or dynamic-dispatch calls attempting to use imported settled-value contracts
selector-only Promise receivers, aggregate combinators, constructors, await, and broad scheduling

covered: settled-value evidence alone is rejected without imported call-target identity
admitted ImportedFunction target plus settled-value evidence resolves the payload NodeId
DirectFunction targets, bad payload anchors, ambiguous records, and conflicting channels are rejected
imported fulfilled .then contracts recover behind a Promise boundary
imported rejected .catch contracts recover behind a Promise boundary
fulfilled contracts with possible thenable payloads remain opaque
source-level imported Promise call-return receivers still report settled-value missing evidence
imported member Promise receivers without settled-value proof remain distinct from direct Promise forms

next_work: Price which imported binding/member Promise candidates are first-party wrappers around Promise.resolve/Promise.reject versus remote I/O or framework producers.
Design a producer for safe first-party wrapper contracts that emits PromiseSettledValue with explicit dependency ids rather than guessing from import names.
Keep aggregate Promise combinators and broad await/scheduling closed until first-settled, all-settled, thenable assimilation, and scheduling obligations are explicit.
